fingerprint: Transitive globe fingerprint via WIN-001 (disc_thickness = Earth's diameter), plus independent spherical fingerprint: the 'cutoff' at 11.7 Hz IS the inter-harmonic minimum of the Schumann spectrum, whose peak/trough structure is determined by the spherical cavity eigenmode equation f_n = (c/2πR)√(n(n+1)) — fully parameterized by R_Earth.

globe_values.name: Schumann eigenmode equation
globe_values.source: Schumann (1952), spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance theory

globe_values.name: Inter-harmonic minimum location
globe_values.value: ~10-12 Hz (between f₁≈7.83 Hz and f₂≈14.3 Hz)
globe_values.source: Standard spherical cavity physics with ionospheric damping; see Nickolaenko & Hayakawa (2002)

dome_values.name: disc resonance ceiling
dome_values.note: Same frequency as WIN-001's f=c/(2D)=11.787 Hz. The dome claims this as both a resonance frequency (WIN-001) and a spectral cutoff (WIN-008) — double-counting one formula.

dome_values.name: disc_thickness (implicit)
dome_values.note: Already identified in WIN-001 fingerprint as Earth's diameter relabeled.

match_precisions.claimed_cutoff_vs_spherical_minimum: The 11.7 Hz 'cutoff' falls within the 10-12 Hz inter-harmonic minimum predicted by spherical cavity theory

why_it_matters: WIN-008 borrows from the globe at two levels. First, the frequency 11.7 Hz derives from disc_thickness = Earth's diameter (already shown in WIN-001). Second, the claimed PHENOMENON — a spectral power minimum at this frequency — is itself predicted by spherical cavity physics. The Schumann eigenfrequencies f_n = (c/2πR)√(n(n+1)) produce peaks at ~7.83, 14.3, 20.8 Hz with minima between them. The minimum near 11-12 Hz exists BECAUSE of the spacing between spherical eigenmodes parameterized by R_Earth. The dome's model provides no mechanism for why spectral power should decrease at 11.7 Hz — its formula f=c/(2D) predicts a resonance (peak), not a minimum. The dome conflates a resonance frequency with a power minimum, then claims the spectral structure that arises from spherical geometry as evidence for its flat disc.
double_counting_note: WIN-008 and WIN-001 use the same formula (f=c/(2D)) and the same implicit parameter (disc_thickness). They are the same claim counted twice: WIN-001 claims the frequency as a resonance, WIN-008 claims the same frequency as a spectral cutoff. The dome gets two WINs from one equation.
suggested_text: The 11.7 Hz 'cutoff' is the same frequency as WIN-001's disc resonance — derived from disc_thickness ≈ Earth's diameter (see WIN-001). But the claimed phenomenon (a spectral power minimum) is independently globe-derived: the Schumann eigenmodes f_n = (c/2πR)√(n(n+1)) place peaks at 7.83 and 14.3 Hz, creating a minimum near 11-12 Hz. The dome's formula predicts a resonance peak, not a power minimum — it borrows the spherical spectral structure while misidentifying its physical meaning.
